Document Name.description: Identifies the contract name — informational only

Parties.description: Names the parties involved — standard clause

Effective Date.description: When terms begin — usually low risk

Renewal Term.description: Auto-renewal terms can trap parties into extended commitments

Notice Period To Terminate Renewal.description: Short notice periods can cause missed cancellation windows

Governing Law.description: Which jurisdiction's laws apply — standard but important

Most Favored Nation.description: Requires matching best terms offered to any party

Non-Compete.description: Restricts future employment or business activities

Exclusivity.description: Prevents working with competitors — limits business freedom

No-Solicit Of Customers.description: Prevents contacting customers after contract ends

No-Solicit Of Employees.description: Prevents hiring employees from the other party

Non-Disparagement.description: Prevents negative public statements — usually mutual

Termination For Convenience.description: Allows one party to end the contract without cause

Rofr/Rofo/Rofn.description: Right of first refusal/offer — limits negotiation flexibility

Change Of Control.description: What happens if a party is acquired or merges

Anti-Assignment.description: Prevents transferring contract rights to third parties

Revenue/Profit Sharing.description: Obligations to share revenue or profits

Price Restrictions.description: Limits on pricing — can affect business strategy

Minimum Commitment.description: Mandatory minimum purchase or service commitment

Volume Restriction.description: Limits on production or transaction volume

Ip Ownership Assignment.description: Transfers intellectual property rights — critical clause

Joint Ip Ownership.description: Shared IP ownership — complex rights situation

License Grant.description: Grants usage rights to intellectual property

Non-Transferable License.description: License cannot be given to others — standard restriction

Unlimited/All-You-Can-Eat License.description: Broad usage rights — generally favorable for licensee

Irrevocable Or Perpetual License.description: License that cannot be taken back — permanent commitment

Source Code Escrow.description: Source code held by third party — protective measure

Post-Termination Services.description: Obligations that continue after contract ends

Audit Rights.description: Right to inspect the other party's records

Uncapped Liability.description: No limit on financial liability — extremely risky

Cap On Liability.description: Limits maximum liability — check if the cap is reasonable

Liquidated Damages.description: Pre-set penalty amounts for breach — can be excessive

Covenant Not To Sue.description: Waives right to take legal action — significant rights waiver

Third Party Beneficiary.description: Third parties who benefit from the contract

Indemnification.description: One party compensates the other for losses — can be very one-sided
